intro: Limonium insigne, also known as Sea Lavender, is a perennial succulent plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its vibrant purple flowers and silvery-gray foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Sea Lavender include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
